mandate: Fix inbox reply UX for mobile and wire escalation drafts to pre-populate the reply composer
keyFindings: InlineReplyComposer had no mobile awareness — fixed font size (16px to prevent iOS zoom), increased textarea rows, larger send button (48px touch target), safe-area-inset-bottom padding

Draft pre-fill was wired to composerInitialText state but only triggered on manual AIDraftCard 'Edit' click — added useEffect on selectedConversationId that auto-detects unsent escalated drafts and pre-fills the composer
whatWorked: The existing composerInitialText + onClearInitial pattern was well-designed for accepting draft text from multiple sources. Adding the useEffect auto-pre-fill slot cleanly into this existing mechanism without any refactoring.
whatFailed: Nothing failed. The changes were surgical and type-checked on first pass.
recommendationsForNextAgent: The 120-char trial limit on replies may truncate AI-drafted escalation replies (which can be longer). Consider either exempting drafts from the limit or truncating with a 'see full draft' expansion. Also the isMobile prop should eventually come from a context rather than prop-drilling through every component.
mannaConsumed: low — single read pass, two file edits, one tsc check
spiritContribution: Made the most emotionally critical moment (couple replying to a sensitive guest message) accessible and frictionless on mobile, with drafts ready to send.
